description: A file in Excel that contains one or more worksheets.
A single sheet within a workbook, made up of rows and columns for organizing data.
The intersection of a row and a column where data, formulas, or functions can be entered.
A horizontal line of cells, identified by numbers (1, 2, 3...).
A vertical line of cells, identified by letters (A, B, C...).
The unique reference of a cell based on its column letter and row number (e.g., A1).
The toolbar that displays and allows editing of data or formulas in the selected cell.
The top panel that contains tabs, groups, and commands for various Excel features.
Categories on the ribbon (e.g., Home, Insert, Formulas) that group related commands.
Displays the address of the active cell and can be used to name or navigate to cells.
The bar at the bottom of Excel showing information such as sum, average, and worksheet view options.
A customizable toolbar that provides easy access to commonly used commands.
Navigation buttons at the bottom of the workbook to switch between worksheets.
Horizontal and vertical sliders to navigate through the worksheet.
The currently selected cell, outlined with a bold border, where input will be placed.
A selection of two or more cells, which can be contiguous or non-contiguous.
An expression used to perform calculations on cell values (e.g., =A1+B1).
A predefined formula that performs specific calculations (e.g., SUM, AVERAGE).
A visual representation of data such as bar, line, or pie charts.
A tool that allows summarizing, analyzing, and organizing large data sets.
The faint lines that separate cells in a worksheet for easier data alignment.
Row numbers and column letters used to identify rows and columns in the worksheet.
A feature to control what kind of data can be entered into a cell.
A feature that applies formatting based on cell values or rules.
Annotations added to cells to provide explanations or context.
A way to record and automate repetitive tasks using VBA.
A tool to display only the rows that meet specific criteria.
A feature to arrange data in ascending or descending order.
Tiny charts within a single cell to show trends in data.
A tool to connect, clean, and transform data from different sources.
An advanced data modeling tool for large datasets and complex calculations.
